title: lessingia glandulifera var. glandulifera
intro: Lessingia glandulifera var. glandulifera, also known as Sticky Sea Lavender, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, Asteraceae, native to coastal areas of California and Baja California. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lessingia glandulifera var. glandulifera include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat skin conditions, coughs, and colds.
